title: [Dragonite] Battle Guide
summary: A [Dragon]/[Flying] setup sweeper whose [multiscale] halves damage at full HP, then finishes games with [dragon-dance] and [extreme-speed].
strengths: [multiscale] halves damage when [Dragonite] enters at full HP
[extreme-speed] provides a rare priority cleaner option
[dragon-dance] plus 134 Attack produces a hard-hitting finisher
weaknesses: 4x [Ice] weakness is a hard structural threat
Loses [multiscale] the moment HP drops below full

recommendedMoves.reason: Key setup move for both Attack and Speed
Decisive [Dragon] STAB for breaking neutral walls

recommendedItems.reason: Absorbs one status move to guarantee the setup turn
Preserves and supports a long-game plan

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Dragonite] is a [Dragon]/[Flying] physical setup sweeper whose entire case is built on the [multiscale] ability. At full HP it takes only half damage from every hit — a free shield on entry — which combined with its 91/95/100 bulk makes it durable enough to set up a [dragon-dance] or two against threats that would shred most other sweepers. With 134 Attack, a +2 priority move in [extreme-speed], and the flexibility to run coverage across [Dragon], [Ground], and [Normal] attacks, [Dragonite] is still one of the most effective win conditions.

## Strengths
[multiscale] is the defining [strength]. One [dragon-dance] turn pushes effective Speed past 120 and effective Attack past 600 damage, enough to two-shot most walls in the format. [extreme-speed] is a rare +2 priority bracket option that turns [Dragonite] into a cleaner even without the setup, and it covers the exact threats that would otherwise [revenge]-kill the slow [Dragonite].

## Weaknesses & Answers
The 4x [Ice] weakness is catastrophic. [stealth-rock] is another structural problem: 25 percent chip on every entry destroys the [multiscale] window entirely, which means hazards must be cleared before [Dragonite] commits. Strong [Fairy] attackers also threaten [Dragonite] via [Fairy]-type coverage like [moonblast] or [play-rough].

## Recommended Moveset
[dragon-dance] is the primary setup move. [extreme-speed] is the locked priority cleaner move that turns [Dragonite] into a [revenge]-killer even at a low HP. [earthquake] covers [Rock], [Steel], and [Electric] walls that would otherwise [block] the [Dragon] STAB, and [outrage] is the main [Dragon] STAB finisher when [Dragonite] needs to punch through neutral walls. [dragon]-claw is an alternate STAB pick that avoids the lock-in but trades raw damage.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[lum-berry] is the default — it absorbs one status attempt (paralysis, sleep, burn) to guarantee the setup turn, which is the single most valuable protection [Dragonite] can get. [leftovers] is the alternate for a long-game [tank] build that preserves [multiscale] through passive regen. The ability must be [multiscale]. Tera [Normal] erases the [Ice] weakness and boosts [extreme-speed] further, and Tera [Steel] flips both [Ice] and [Dragon] weaknesses into resistances at the cost of STAB damage.

## Teammates & Synergy
A hazard remover is mandatory. An [Ice] resist ([Steel] or [Fire] partner) covers the biggest structural threat, and a [wish] user on the back line restores [Dragonite]'s full HP so [multiscale] reactivates during a mid-match swap. Physical breakers covering [Fairy] and [Dragon] walls pair with [Dragonite]'s sweep plan.

## Play Patterns
The baseline plan is to clear hazards, bring [Dragonite] in on a predicted switch, dance on the swap turn, then start chaining [outrage] and [earthquake] based on the incoming target. [extreme-speed] should be held for a late-game [revenge] on a weakened cleaner. Save the Tera specifically for the moment an [Ice] move is about to land.
